title: Republic Army
content:
{{Top|canon=Republic army}}
{{Update|[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]], [[Star Wars: The Old Republic, Threat of Peace]]}}

The '''Republic Army''', previously known as the '''Republic Armies''',<ref name="KOTORCG">''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]''</ref> was the collective ground forces of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] from its founding in [[25,053 BBY]] until the dissolution of the [[Republic Military/Legends|Republic Military]] in [[1000 BBY/Legends|1000 BBY]]. A force with a long history, its forces included specialized troops such as [[Rocket-Jumper Elite Advance Unit|rocket-jumpers]] and also a fair number of combat droids of differing models.

==History==
===The Sith Wars===
From its beginning, the growing Republic established its own military forces to defend its borders. [[Soldier/Legends|Soldiers]] from Republic planets joined the ranks of the newly formed army. Like the [[Republic Navy/Legends|Navy]], the Army grew quickly to meet the incredible demands of simultaneously defending the Republic and maintaining order within its borders.<ref name="Star Wars: The Old Republic Encyclopedia" /> The Army existed as far back as [[4000 BBY/Legends|4000 BBY]]. At some point, the Army established a military academy on the planet [[Carida/Legends|Carida]].<ref name="HNN53147">[[HoloNet News Vol. 531 47|''HoloNet News Vol. 531'' #47]]</ref> Maintenance of the Armies along with the [[Republic Navy/Legends|Republic Navy]] was handled by the [[Minister of Defense (Republic)|Minister of Defense]] in the aftermath of the [[Great Hyperspace War/Legends|Great Hyperspace War]].<ref name="TJC">''[[Tales of the Jedi Companion]]''</ref> Following that time, Brigadiers of the Republic Armies were known to have participated in the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]]. At the start of the [[Mandalorian Wars]], there were multiple armies in different sectors of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. They were only united under one banner when Supreme Chancellor Tol Cressa named [[Revan/Legends|Revan]] commander of the Republic's military forces.<ref name="EG Warfare" /><ref name="KOTORCG" /> A large number of these soldiers later turned traitor and became [[Sith trooper (Sith Empire)|Sith troopers]] when [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Revan, the hero of the Mandalorian Wars, returned from the [[Unknown Regions/Legends|Unknown Regions]] and formed a [[Darth Revan's Sith Empire|Sith Empire]]. These former [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] soldiers were heavily involved in the [[Jedi Civil War]] and the resultant [[Dark Wars]]. The new unified Republic Army later served to combat the reborn Sith Empire that instigated the [[Great Galactic War]] and also continued to protect the Republic's reduced territory in the [[Cold War (Galactic Republic–Sith Empire)|Cold War]] that followed the conflict.<ref name="TOR">''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]''</ref>

The Republic Army was heavily engaged in the [[New Sith Wars]] from [[2000 BBY/Legends|2000]] to [[1000 BBY/Legends|1000 BBY]]. The Army suffered one of the worst defeats in its history in [[1466 BBY]] when the [[New Sith]] crushed Republic and [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] forces in the [[Battle of Mizra]]. This bloody conflict was later immortalized by the poet [[Felloux]].<ref name="The New Essential Chronology">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> Over five hundred thousand soldiers died in the battle, and hundreds of Jedi converted to the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]].<ref name="The Essential Atlas">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> Around 1010 BBY, during the last years of the New Sith Wars, the Republic Army was incorporated into the Jedi-led [[Army of Light/Legends|Army of Light]] under the command of [[Jedi Lord|Lord]] [[Hoth (Jedi Lord)|Hoth]]. In a grueling [[Ruusan campaign|campaign]] on the planet [[Ruusan/Legends|Ruusan]], the Army of Light slowly ground down the Sith [[Brotherhood of Darkness/Legends|Brotherhood of Darkness]] until it finally achieved a costly victory at the [[Seventh Battle of Ruusan]].

===Dissolution and reformation===
Following the end of the New Sith Wars in [[1000 BBY/Legends|1000 BBY]], the [[Ruusan Reformations/Legends|Ruusan Reformation]] eliminated the Republic's military, with only a few remaining ground military units were placed under the jurisdiction of the [[Judicial Forces/Legends|Judicial Forces]]. The Republic came to rely more heavily on [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]] and local [[Planetary Security Forces]] for keeping peace in the [[Galaxy/Legends|galaxy]]. In [[44 BBY/Legends|44 BBY]] during the [[Stark Hyperspace War/Legends|Stark Hyperspace War]], [[Ranulph Tarkin/Legends|Ranulph Tarkin]] tried to portray the ground forces of [[Outland Regions Security Force/Legends|his fleet]] as the legitimate Republic Army, even referring to it as such. In the final years of the Republic, the Army had degenerated into a poorly trained police force that simply wore fancy uniforms.<ref name="ISB">''[[Imperial Sourcebook]]''</ref>

At the time of the [[Separatist Crisis/Legends|Separatist Crisis]], a number of [[Militarists/Legends|militarist]] [[Senator/Legends|senators]] pushed for the passage of the [[Military Creation Act/Legends|Military Creation Act]], a [[Galactic Senate/Legends|legislative]] bill that would reconstitute the Republic Army and Navy as a pan-galactic military force. When the Senate learned of the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Separatists]]' plot to gain independence via [[Separatist Droid Army/Legends|military force]],<ref name="EpisodeII">{{Film|II}}</ref> the Republic Army was reestablished for the first time in a millennium.<ref name="EG Warfare" /> However, the Republic also authorized the establishment of another branch of the [[Republic Military/Legends|military]], an army of [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]] known as the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]].<ref name="EpisodeII" />

===The Clone Wars===
{{Main|Grand Army of the Republic/Legends}}
[[File:CoruscantCloneTroopers-AOTC.png|thumb|left|250px|The Grand Army of the Republic at the beginning of the Clone Wars]]The Grand Army of the Republic was a major branch of the Galactic Republic Military composed entirely of clone [[Trooper/Legends|troopers]], an [[Army/Legends|army]] of elite soldiers [[Cloning/Legends|created]] from the [[DNA/Legends|genetically]] altered [[Clone template/Legends|template]] of the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]].<ref name="EpisodeII" />

The Republic's armies also contained non-clone units. Though most [[HoloNet/Legends|HoloNet]]-documented battles were fought by the all-clone Grand Army and their [[Jedi General/Legends|Jedi officers]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], the non-clone Republic Army engaged in many battles throughout the galaxy without the aid of a single clone trooper. While clone and non-clone units sometimes served side by side, coordinating the two proved difficult; with the exception of [[Republic officer/Legends|Republic officers]] who were assigned to lead clone units, most non-clone troops served in the Planetary Security Forces (which were largely used in defensive deployments), or with the Judicials.<ref name="EG Warfare" />

During the war, [[Judicial/Legends|Judicial]] [[Terrinald Screed/Legends|Terrinald Screed]] voiced his opinion on the HoloNet that the Republic Army and Navy should fall under a unified command directed from Coruscant,<ref name="HNNV:53147">[[HoloNet News Vol. 531 47|''HoloNet News Vol. 531'' #47]]</ref> an idea that was realized by the Republic's successor state, the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]].<ref name="EpisodeIII">{{Film|III}}</ref> Under the [[New Order/Legends|New Order]], the Republic Army was reformed into the [[Imperial Army/Legends|Imperial Army]] while the clone troopers were reorganized into the [[Stormtrooper Corps/Legends|Stormtrooper Corps]].<ref name="EG Warfare" /> Many elements of the Republic Army, such as the Special Missions units, were adopted by the Imperial Army and expanded to create a much more formidable military force.<ref name="RoE:TRSH">''[[Rules of Engagement: The Rebel SpecForce Handbook]]''</ref>

==Organization==
===Administration===
{{Quote|We'd rather rent than own.|[[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Veltraa]] on the Republic Army|<ref name="KOTORCG" />}}
[[File:JumpinRockets.jpg|thumb|150px|[[Rocket-Jumper Elite Advance Unit|Republic rocket-jumpers]], a faction of the Republic Army in 3,998 BBY]]
As the early Republic Armies consisted of local militias and mercenaries with no true command in place, its generals often received orders straight from the [[Ministry of Defense|Republic Defense Ministry]], and no single surface marshal coordinated or directed its operations. This often left individual army units ultimately under the control of the [[Republic Navy/Legends|Republic Navy]] and led to many soldiers being stationed more on their starships than on planetary surfaces. This meant that in wartime scenarios, members of the Republic Armies tended to be in transit aboard starships rather than stationed on planets.<ref name="KOTORCG" />

The chaotic nature of this piecemeal formation meant that there was no uniformity within the Republic Armies, and not all of its individual units were created equally. In fact, units did not possess any uniform designation but rather their original nomenclatures. Its structure tended to possess both local and historical eccentricities, though there were elements common amongst the various military divisions. Each Army division was associated with particular places or beings. For example, a division raised on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] might adopt (or be given) that name as part of its title, thereby becoming the Coruscant Division.<ref name="KOTORCG" /> One part of the Republic Army that outlived the rest was the [[Carida Academy/Legends|Academy of Carida]], which served as one of its officer training schools.<ref name="Hearing">{{WEGCite|book=Dark Empire Sourcebook|story=The Hearing}}</ref>

This meant that the Armies exerted very little political influence in the Republic. Despite this, the Navy showed little desire to incorporate the Armies units as marines with themselves as the controlling body. This tended to form a sometimes violent cultural clash between the two divisions but also meant that the biggest expense faced by the Army, such as the movement of their vehicles, was placed on the Navy's budget.<ref name="KOTORCG" />

With pay and conditions being far worse than those of the Navy, the Army surface units tended to suffer from severe recruitment problems. In order to combat this problem, the Republic began contracting any existing local militias or even mercenary units, sometimes buying them out entirely. A further incentive for dangerous assignments was the offer of healthy signing bonuses for those that accepted entry into the Republic Armies. Though service was not compulsory, it was known that the irregular units tended to make use of recruiting methods that the Defense Ministry did not want to know about.<ref name="KOTORCG" /> In some cases, infantry troops were regarded as archaic holdovers from a less “civilized” era of warfare.<ref name="Star Wars: The Old Republic Encyclopedia" />

After the Jedi Civil War, the reliance on Jedi and the critical shortcomings of the Armies led to prioritization of a reliable unified military.<ref name="Star Wars: The Old Republic Encyclopedia">''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic Encyclopedia]]''</ref> The Republic Army had become fully integrated and professional by the time of the Cold War, with the [[Personnel Division of the Army of the Galactic Republic]] being an army-wide organization responsible for staff issues, and the [[Republic Special Forces Division]] being responsible for [[Special Operations]].<ref name="TOR" /> A logistical network was established to provide the Army with constant support and reinforcements.<ref name="Star Wars: The Old Republic Encyclopedia" />

The Army suffered financially during the last years of the Old Republic, which hindered the procurement of equipment. A common factor that the Republic Armies suffered from was its reliance on the Navy for not only transportation but orbital support, though only lip service was ever provided in times of conflict. In addition, the Navy often jealously withheld the support of its starfighter squadrons, which denied the Army the capacity to maintain an orbit free of enemy fighters after the fleet had left.<ref name="ISB" />

===Tactical organization===
[[File:RepublicTroopersKotORCG.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Soldiers battling the Sith]]
During the era of the war against the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorians]], it was officially believed that there was no rank of lieutenant, captain or colonel present within the Armies in order to prevent any confusion with the Naval positions. However, on a vestigial level, it existed amongst the irregular native units along with a number of stranger titles including commandant, Gray Chieftain and Grand Xenog.<ref name="KOTORCG" />

The Armies were divided according to local historical structure, but common structures were:
*'''Squad''' : consisting of 8 to 12 infantry that were led by both a sergeant and a corporal<ref name="KOTORCG" />
*'''Battalion''' : consisting of 25 to 40 squads that were led by a major
*'''Brigade''' : ranging between four and nine battalions that had the capacity to hold from 1,000 to 5,000 soldiers. It was led by a brigadier with its own headquarters unit with their mission roles involving large-scale landing operations as well as raids and occupation. Their command and control was directed from Navy vessels if present.<ref name="KOTORCG" />
*'''Corps''' : these consisted of all forces present in the region and were named after the planet which served as their headquarters. A general served as its leader, though they simply just directed the orders from the Defense Ministry to the individual brigadiers.<ref name="KOTORCG" />
The Republic Army in the Great Galactic War had established a permanent command structure:<ref name="Star Wars: The Old Republic Encyclopedia" />

*'''Fire Team''' : 2 troops
*'''Squad''' : 6–10 troops
*'''Platoon''' : 50–100 troops
*'''Company''' : 150–250 troops
*'''Battalion''' : 750–1,500 troops
*'''Regiment''' : 4,000–6,000 troops
*'''Division''' : 20,000–40,000 troops. Divisions were the largest force that could typically deployed by a fleet, thus the largest unit generally used in operations.
*'''Task Force''' : 40,000+ troops. Task Forces were temporary groupings of Divisions formed when a single division was inadequate.

In the last days of the Republic, there was a clear distinction between combat troops and support as well as command elements within the Army. As such, only the soldiers appeared on the battlefield and engaged the enemy, while the command as well as support personnel remained far removed from the combat. This meant that it was rare to see an individual over the rank of lieutenant present in blaster combat.<ref name="ISB" />

==Equipment==
Republic soldiers were decently equipped and supported by several kinds of aerial vehicles. They bore both [[Grenade/Legends|grenades]] and [[Blaster/Legends|blasters]]. The chaos of the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]] led to the deliberate reintroduction of melee weapons. This was brought about by the abundance of [[Sith/Legends|lightsaber-wielding opponents]]. To guard against these weapons, Republic swords were required to be made with a [[Cortosis/Legends|cortosis]] weave, often of [[Echani/Legends|Echani]] origin. Armor was worn and was noted for its modular nature. One characteristic of the Republic military was its equipping of its personnel with an array of cybernetic implants and headwear. Some of these were deliberately designed to provide mental protection against the powers of [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] opponents.

Another trait was its use of specialty equipment, such as the [[Stealth field generator/Legends|stealth field generator]], and also physical enhancement technology. These were non-combat items and saw considerable research and development as a result of the Republic's use. As of the Jedi Civil War, personal shield generators were used. Some models were purchased and some were manufactured directly for the Republic. However, the cost of these devices was such that only small units could be assured of a continual supply. Strike teams favored them as they enabled them to outfight an entire unit with conventional gear. The Republic Army also made use of defensive devices such as turrets and a variety of land mines. This latter item ranged from fragmentation to plasma to toxin, though the first was more common due to how expensive deadly toxins were.

The Republic Army made use of [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droids]] amongst their forces to augment their fighting potential, with the [[Sentinel droid]] being one common version used for a time.

During the Cold War, Republic infantry soldiers were equipped with lightweight [[Duraplast/Legends|duraplast]] armor, a [[Blaster rifle/Legends|blaster rifle]], a [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster pistol]], and grenades, and carried four days of rations. Rifles of the Republic Army all had integrated grenade launchers, and the rifle could be loaded with a variety of different types of ammunition. Special Forces troopers had access to [[Durasteel/Legends|durasteel]] and cortosis armor, handheld [[Blaster cannon/Legends|blaster cannons]], and personal shield generators.<ref name="Star Wars: The Old Republic Encyclopedia" />

==Personnel==
===Specializations===
The most basic non-specialized trooper in the Republic Army was the [[Republic soldier]], who typically wore full heavy body [[Armor/Legends|armor]] and were armed with [[Blaster rifle/Legends|blaster rifles]] during the [[Great Galactic War|Galactic Wars]].<ref name="TOR" /> Republic Guards were those soldiers assigned to duty on Republic Navy [[Warship/Legends|warships]] as [[marine]]s.<ref name="WizardsCite-''Knights of the Old Republic'' Miniatures Preview 4">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/KOTORminispreview4|text=''Knights of the Old Republic'' Miniatures Preview 4}}</ref>

The Republic Army maintained a number of [[Special forces/Legends|special forces]] units throughout its history. One of the most glamorous and storied elite units was the [[Rocket-Jumper Elite Advance Unit]]. The Rocket-Jumpers used [[Jetpack/Legends|jetpacks]] to penetrate deep into enemy territory, using low-altitude jumps to avoid enemy forces and then setting up airheads, staging areas where follow-up troops and heavy equipment could be shuttled in.<ref name="Star Wars: The Essential Guide to Warfare Author's Cut">{{SW|url=news/the-essential-guide-to-warfare-authors-cut-part-4-the-rise-of-the-republic-needs-author-entry|series=Star Wars: The Essential Guide to Warfare Author's Cut|text=Part 4: The Rise of the Republic}}</ref>

Another special operations unit was the Army Recon Commandos of the [[Mandalorian Wars]]. These were descended from mercenary units that were skilled in special missions that required multitasking and were often fitted for a variety of multiple environment specializations. These soldiers were grouped together into reconnaissance squads that were often inserted onto the battlefield in order to gather intelligence on enemy positions. This was not their only purpose, as Recon Commandos often engaged tactical control operations and were employed to position combat squads. In addition, these soldiers were tasked with covert ops such as sabotage or assisting Jedi in the field. Those inducted into the ranks of the Recon Commandos were often extremely busy as their talents were greatly used.<ref name="KOTORCG" />

During the Cold War, Republic soldiers went through a grueling eighteen-week basic training course, followed by up to twelve months of specialist training.<ref name="Star Wars: The Old Republic Encyclopedia" />

===Ranks===
[[File:Kotor_manaan_rep_soldier.jpg|thumb|right|150px|A Republic soldier]]
Ranks within the Mandalorian Wars–era Republic Army included:
*'''Army Brigadier''': The highest-ranking officers within the Republic military that were deployed in the field. During the age of the Jedi Civil War, these brigadiers were often challenged with adequately supplying their troopers on the battlefield, though certain clever individuals often had their forces live off the land. They were also not noted for their negotiation skills, which was a hindrance in operations that did not require any military action. Some brigadiers sabotaged their own careers in a way that did not harm their troops in order to avoid being promoted to full general and thus being burdened by the responsibilities of that post.<ref name="KOTORCG" />
*'''Army Officer''': These field commanders often held the rank of sergeant or corporal and descended from local militia's mercenary groups before they joined the Republic army. This made them the brightest as well as the best officers available. During the time of the Jedi Civil War, these officers were extensively trained in the use of [[Vibro-weapon/Legends|vibroweapons]] after it was recognized that [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]]-wielding [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] often targeted these soldiers in order to eliminate their leadership of their units.<ref name="KOTORCG" />
*'''Army Trooper''': The heart of the Republic's army consisted of squads of army troopers who were kept in close quarters to the point that they considered themselves all friends. Commanders often kept these individuals together to encourage camaraderie and improve the units' performance. However, logistical problems at the time led to the Republic Army not recognizing the special skills of individuals troopers and not assigning them to squads that would benefit from their abilities.<ref name="KOTORCG" />
The Republic Army during the Cold War utilized ranks of:<ref name="Star Wars: The Old Republic Encyclopedia" />

*'''Grand Marshal'''<ref name="TOR" />
*'''General'''
*'''Colonel'''
*'''Major'''
*'''Captain'''
*'''Lieutenant'''
*'''Ensign'''
*'''Sergeant'''
*'''Corporal'''
*'''Specialist'''
*'''Private'''

"Commander" was not a rank in the Cold War–era Republic Army, but was a traditional honorific showing exceptional respect to veteran officers of superior leadership.<ref name="Star Wars: The Old Republic Encyclopedia" />
